small_step_id: Year 1_5-6_Autumn__Addition and subtraction within 10_5_Number bonds within 10
baseline_ss_wr_desc: combine their knowledge of the part-whole model and addition facts to explore number bonds within 10 Starting with the whole, children break numbers into parts and explore how many different ways a number can be partitioned. Double-sided counters and ten frames are useful concrete resources, together with dot patterns. Children will see numbers made from dot patterns differently, for example some may see 6 as being made up of 5 and 1, while others may see it as being made up of two 3s. Exploring patterns is a good way to encourage discussion and expose children to different ways of thinking. Throughout this step, continue to look at number sentences written with the symbols in different places and talk about the commutative nature of the calculations, for example 3 + 1 = 4 is the same as 1 + 3 = 4
title: Parts and Whole - Learn To Break Numbers Into Parts
instruction_justification: The transcript effectively introduces the concept of parts and wholes using relatable language and visual aids like snap cubes and number bonds, which align well with the target curriculum objective. It maintains focus on the single concept of number bonds within 10, uses simple language appropriate for young learners, and includes engaging prompts for student participation. However, it could benefit from slightly clearer definitions of terms like 'parts' and 'whole' to further enhance understanding.

title: Number bonds to 20 | Maths for kids | DoodleLearning
instruction_justification: The transcript introduces the concept of number bonds but focuses on bonds to 20 instead of the target objective of bonds within 10, which may confuse young learners. While it uses 10 frames and attempts to engage students with prompts, the language is somewhat complex for the age group, and the explanation lacks clarity and simplicity needed for effective understanding.

title: Maths for kids- 2 Digit addition using number bonds
instruction_justification: The transcript attempts to explain number bonds but lacks clarity and focus, making it difficult for young learners to grasp the concept. Additionally, the explanations are not structured in a way that promotes engagement or understanding of the core concept.
